examples.en: Dancing stimulates the brain like little else.
tips: 連結: little‿else=リトゥルエルス(L連結)
弱形: the を弱く
強勢: DAN·cing, STI·mulates, BRAIN, LIT·tle
注意音: stim-の/st/クラスター, littleのt(米=フラップ/英=明瞭)

examples.en: Despite some uncertainty, one theory is that a key factor is how music and movement combine to generate dopamine.
examples.linking: Despite some‿uncertainty, one theory‿is that‿a key factor‿is how music‿and movement combine to generate dopamine.
tips: 連結: factor‿is=ファクタリズ, music‿and
弱形: some/that/a/and(=/ən/)/to(=/tə/)
強勢: uncer·TAIN·ty, THEO·ry, FAC·tor, GE·nerate, DO·pamine
注意音: TH(theory/that), R(theory/factor)

examples.en: One part of the puzzle is straightforward: there is mounting evidence that the brain loves to spot patterns and to mirror others.
Despite some uncertainty, one theory is that a key factor is how music and movement combine to generate dopamine.

examples.en: The brain loves to predict patterns, so watching a complex move we cannot foresee creates an intense rush of pleasure — it is obvious why we love the dance floor.
examples.linking: The brain loves to predict patterns, so watching‿a complex move we cannot foresee creates‿an‿intense rush‿of pleasure — it‿is‿obvious why we love the dance floor.
tips: 連結: watching‿a=ワッチンガ, rush‿of=ラッショヴ, it‿is=イティズ
弱形: to/a/an/of/the
強勢: pre·DICT, PAT·terns, com·PLEX, in·TENSE, OB·vious
注意音: R(predict/rush/floor), pleasureの/ʒ/

passage.jp: ダンスは他に類を見ないほど脳を刺激する。多少の不確かさはあるが、一説では、音楽と動きが組み合わさってドーパミンを生み出す点が重要な要因だという。脳はパターンを予測するのが好きで、予見できない複雑な動きを見ると強烈な快感が湧く——私たちがダンスフロアを愛する理由は明らかだ。
